response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned engineer with genuine Staff-level experience, a strong distributed-systems track record (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and low-latency design), and current hands-on work on AI-agentic infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq — directly relevant nice-to-haves. However, Python does not appear anywhere in the resume or skills list despite being a stated must-have, which is a significant gap for this role. TypeScript depth is solid, and the breadth of seniority and community contributions (workshops, conference talks, open-source maintainer) signals the technical-strategy mindset the role requires. The Python absence and thin evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management keep this a conditional recommendation pending clarification.
strengths: Direct high-throughput distributed systems experience: Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of HTTP req/s with async I/O, fibers, and low-latency caching
Current AI/agentic infrastructure work including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq, satisfying the AI-native environment nice-to-haves
Consistent Staff Engineer designation across multiple roles, community knowledge-sharing (workshops, FOSS4G talk), and open-source project ownership demonstrating technical-leadership depth
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume and skills list despite being listed as a must-have requirement; this is a hard gap that needs direct clarification before proceeding

No explicit evidence of stakeholder management across product, engineering, and leadership functions — the resume is IC/craft-focused with little signal on cross-functional coordination or influencing without authority

Operational ownership and on-call readiness are not documented; the role requires full operational accountability including an out-of-hours rotation, and the resume gives no indication of prior incident-response or production ownership experience
